name: Kurt Weill
abstract: Kurt Julian Weill (; German: [vaɪl]; March 2, 1900 – April 3, 1950) was a German-born American composer active from the 1920s in his native country, and in his later years in the United States. He was a leading composer for the stage who was best known for his fruitful collaborations with Bertolt Brecht.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I did Covent Garden with the Royal Opera, and I did The Seven Deadly Sins. ... I did – I’ll show you a poster of it, for The Threepenny Opera. ... I was playing the part that Kurt Weill had written for Lotte Lenya.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: Well in 1990 I did a show in Germany, it was a Kurt Weill show for his, I think his 90th birthday for the WDR band in Cologne.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I mean we were playing like a Kurt Weill’s tune, a Kurt Weill’s tune today at the jazz camp
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: sort of like – you’ve seen or you’ve heard some of Kurt Weill’s music – that idiom.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
